title: [InstCombine]: Fold OR bit tests
author: arrowten
description:
Fold: OR of bit tests with shifted constants.
((X & (C0 << Y)) != 0) || ((X & (C1 << Y)) != 0) --> (X & ((C0 | C1) << Y)) != 0

tests.test_body:
define i1 @src(i64 %arg0, ptr %arg1) {
  %v0 = load i8, ptr %arg1, align 1
  %v1 = trunc nuw nsw i64 %arg0 to i32
  %v2 = shl nuw nsw i32 1, %v1
  %v3 = trunc nuw nsw i32 %v2 to i8
  %v4 = and i8 %v0, %v3
  %v5 = shl nuw nsw i32 4, %v1
  %v6 = trunc nuw nsw i32 %v5 to i8
  %v7 = and i8 %v0, %v6
  %v8 = icmp ne i8 %v4, 0
  %v9 = icmp ne i8 %v7, 0
  %v10 = select i1 %v8, i1 true, i1 %v9
  ret i1 %v10
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:

comments.author: Explorer09
comments.body:
As I mentioned in the original report (#167199). This pattern should be a multi-step reduction. I don't think it's appropriate to implement directly from `((x & (c0<<y)) != 0) || ((x & (c1<<y)) != 0)` to `((x & ((c0|c1)<<y)) != 0)`.

```text
((x & (c0<<y)) != 0) || ((x & (c1<<y)) != 0)
→ (((x & (c0<<y)) | (x & (c1<<y))) != 0)
→ ((x & ((c0<<y) | (c1<<y))) != 0)
→ ((x & ((c0|c1)<<y)) != 0)
```

```text
(a != 0 || b != 0) → (a | b) != 0 // when accessing b has no side effects
(a & b | a & c) → (a & (b | c))
((a << y) | (b << y)) → (a | b) << y
```

Yeah. That should be three rules.

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> (a != 0 || b != 0) → (a | b) != 0

This doesn't hold for logical or. If you change the logical or into a bitwise one, InstCombine already converts the source pattern into the expected one: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/zvjTuU

According to the original motivating pattern:
```
define i1 @src(i64 %arg0, ptr %arg1) {
  %v0 = load i8, ptr %arg1, align 1
  %v1 = trunc nuw nsw i64 %arg0 to i32
  %v2 = shl nuw nsw i32 1, %v1
  %v3 = trunc nuw nsw i32 %v2 to i8
  %v4 = and i8 %v0, %v3
  %v5 = shl nuw nsw i32 4, %v1
  %v6 = trunc nuw nsw i32 %v5 to i8
  %v7 = and i8 %v0, %v6
  %v8 = icmp ne i8 %v4, 0
  %v9 = icmp ne i8 %v7, 0
  %v10 = select i1 %v8, i1 true, i1 %v9
  ret i1 %v10
}
```
If we can prove `%v9` is poison implies that `%v8` is either poison or true, InstCombine will convert the logical or into a bitwise or and unblock subsequent optimizations.
